document.title: AKTI ERP Phase 2 Hardening Ticket Pack
document.revision_label_policy: Revision label v1.3; active repo filename remains v1.
document.purpose: Define the complete autonomous Phase 2 hardening run from P2H-000 through P2H-014, ending with a final external audit package generated from committed branch HEAD.
corrections_applied: Normalized active repo paths to v1 filenames.
Set revision label to v1.3 while keeping active repo filenames at v1.
Resolved queue-end mismatch: the hardening queue runs P2H-000 through P2H-014.
Defined P2H-014 as the final external audit package ticket.
Required P2H-000 to make hardening docs tracked repo authority before implementation tickets.
Clarified postmortem files as source-file context only unless present and committed.
Clarified final audit artifact commit behavior.
Made final package steps capture FULL_SHA and SHORT_SHA before use.
Added unzip and checksum utility availability checks for final packaging.
Added frontend no-new-dependency test strategy and hard stop for missing tooling.
Added ADR/subdecision rule for material outbox retry/dead-letter architecture changes.
Clarified pass grouping as explanatory only.
Tightened broad scopes with exact-file implementation planning before edits.

run_contract.start_point: Create or use a hardening branch from the latest Phase 2 autonomous branch HEAD after P2H-000 makes the hardening docs tracked repo authority.
run_contract.ticket_order_policy: Run P2H-000 through P2H-014 in exact order. Do not skip, reorder, or merge tickets unless the pack is corrected first.
run_contract.doc_tracking_rule: P2H-000 must make docs/process/AKTI_ERP_Phase_2_Hardening_Audit_Report_v1.md and docs/process/AKTI_ERP_Phase_2_Hardening_Ticket_Pack_v1.json tracked repo files and commit them before implementation tickets begin.
run_contract.schema_gate_rule: No schema-changing ticket may run before P2H-001 resolves migration policy.
run_contract.failure_model: Codex must stop honestly on hard gates and preserve artifacts; do not broaden scope to pass validation.
run_contract.pass_group_policy: Passes are explanatory only and have no execution authority. The ordered_ticket_queue array is authoritative.
run_contract.postmortem_rule: If a Phase 2 postmortem file is unavailable in the repo, it is source-file context only and must not be treated as repo authority.
run_contract.artifact_rule: Each implementation ticket creates artifacts before commit. If codex-review is ignored, artifacts remain uncommitted but must be included in the final external audit package and journaled.

source_of_truth_order: chat/uploaded files as supporting context only
global_non_scope: No production auth/session unless separately scoped by ADR/ticket
No direct Lead Desk to Meta/WhatsApp API calls
No LMS, HR, Finance, Hiring, Certification, Website Builder, or AI content editor
No invented modules, capabilities, permissions, roles, events, screens, or business rules
No broad schema redesign outside planned tickets

global_rules.stronger_path_default: Within approved Phase 2 scope, implement the stronger concrete runtime path by default. Use documented deferral only when behavior is truly out of Phase 2 scope or requires production-only infrastructure.
global_rules.exact_file_plan_rule: Before editing each ticket, produce an exact-file implementation plan from the ticket scope. Stop if required files fall outside the exact plan.
global_rules.bounded_repair_rule: For deterministic validation failures inside the active ticket scope, Codex may perform up to three repair cycles and must journal each repair.
global_rules.validation_wiring_rule: If a ticket adds tests, scoped package/script wiring required to run those tests is allowed only when listed in expected files or exact-file plan.
global_rules.runtime_consistency_rule: Hardening must close manifest capability to seed to assignment to runtime permission, manifest event to outbox to registry to tests, screen contract to frontend to tests, and Gatekeeper contract to runtime provider to service tests.
global_rules.whatsapp_boundary_rule: Lead Desk must not call Meta/WhatsApp directly. Stub/non-production WhatsApp integration must remain mediated through Engagement Gateway Lite.
global_rules.artifact_rule: Each ticket writes ticket artifacts and appends the hardening journal before committing source changes. Final ticket creates external audit package.
global_rules.broad_glob_rule: Broad globs are inspection hints only. They do not grant edit permission until converted into an exact-file implementation plan.
global_rules.frontend_test_rule: Because apps/web currently has a scaffold test script, P2H-012 must use available no-new-dependency tooling for meaningful tests or stop for explicit dependency/tooling approval.
global_rules.outbox_architecture_rule: If P2H-005 changes outbox retry/dead-letter behavior beyond foundation metadata, Codex must create or reference an ADR/subdecision before implementation continues.
global_rules.stronger_path_authority_rule: The stronger implementation path must be derived only from Prisma, contracts, manifests, generated registry, ADRs, and active control docs. If a stronger path requires a new business rule, capability, permission, role, event, screen, module, or architecture decision, stop.

stop_conditions: unsafe execution environment
required file outside exact ticket scope
new dependency required but not approved
secret or production credential needed
production deployment required
production auth/session required outside explicit ticket scope
destructive migration required
schema-changing work attempted before P2H-001
migration strategy remains unclear for a schema-changing ticket
direct Lead Desk to Meta/WhatsApp coupling appears
real outbound WhatsApp delivery required
invented module, capability, permission, role, event, screen, or business rule required
out-of-scope module required
frontend screen lacks approved screen contract
apps/web meaningful tests require a new dependency and no approval exists
validation failure persists after three bounded repairs
hardening docs are not tracked after P2H-000
ticket artifacts cannot be created or verified
final audit package cannot be generated or verified
broad glob cannot be converted into exact-file implementation plan
required final packaging tool is unavailable and no documented fallback exists
P2H-014 attempts to package anything other than committed branch HEAD

ordered_ticket_queue.title: Housekeeping, source-doc tracking, and baseline lock
ordered_ticket_queue.objective: Make the hardening docs tracked repo authority, lock the branch baseline, and record current validation state before implementation begins.
scope: Confirm current branch, commit, and working tree state.
Confirm the active hardening docs exist at docs/process/AKTI_ERP_Phase_2_Hardening_Audit_Report_v1.md and docs/process/AKTI_ERP_Phase_2_Hardening_Ticket_Pack_v1.json.
If hardening docs are untracked, stage and commit them before implementation tickets run.
If only source-revision files exist, normalize them into the active v1 repo paths before commit.
If the Phase 2 postmortem is unavailable in the repo, treat it as source-file context only.
Record whether P2C-GATE was artifact-only and what final Phase 2 package exists.
Create initial hardening journal location.
ordered_ticket_queue.permission_model: No permission model change unless explicitly stated in ticket scope.
ordered_ticket_queue.gatekeeper_policy: No Gatekeeper behavior change unless explicitly stated in ticket scope.
ordered_ticket_queue.audit_outbox_policy: No audit/outbox behavior change unless explicitly stated in ticket scope.
stop_conditions: hardening docs cannot be normalized into active v1 repo paths
branch cannot be identified
working tree has unrelated tracked changes
baseline validation cannot be run and failure is not documented
ordered_ticket_queue.rollback_notes: If the only changes are docs/process hardening files and journal setup, commit those as the baseline hardening commit.

ordered_ticket_queue.title: Prisma migration policy and non-destructive baseline
ordered_ticket_queue.objective: Resolve migration policy before any schema-changing hardening work.
scope: Inspect current Prisma schema and absence/presence of prisma/migrations.
Decide baseline migration path using repo reality.
If migrations are in scope, create non-destructive migration artifacts for current schema state.
If migrations remain deferred, create a formal ADR/subdecision stating schema deployment is not merge-ready until migration baseline is created.
Replace invalid migration diff fallback commands with executable commands plus explicit unavailable-command reporting.
non_scope: No live migration execution against production.
files_expected_to_change: prisma/migrations/** if non-destructive baseline is generated
docs/process/AKTI_ERP_Phase_2_Hardening_Ticket_Pack_v1.json only if validation command text must be corrected by explicit control-doc correction
files_forbidden_to_change: packages/contracts/** except references required by validation
ordered_ticket_queue.permission_model: No permission model change unless explicitly stated in ticket scope.
ordered_ticket_queue.gatekeeper_policy: No Gatekeeper behavior change unless explicitly stated in ticket scope.
ordered_ticket_queue.audit_outbox_policy: No audit/outbox behavior change unless explicitly stated in ticket scope.
stop_conditions: migration strategy remains unclear
migration would be destructive
production DB access is required
schema change needed outside declared scope
ordered_ticket_queue.rollback_notes: Revert this ticket commit and its ticket artifacts if acceptance criteria are not met.

ordered_ticket_queue.title: Phase 2 CI and validation gate
ordered_ticket_queue.objective: Make CI and local validation Phase 2-aware before further runtime/schema hardening proceeds.
scope: Update or add workflow so Phase 2 validation is explicit.
Run registry:verify:phase2 rather than using Phase 1 verification as the final Phase 2 gate.
Ensure contracts, screen contracts, Prisma validate/generate, registry generate/check, lint, typecheck, test, and build are wired.
Keep Phase 1 checks only if they remain valid as subset checks.
non_scope: No broad workflow redesign unrelated to Phase 2 validation.
No dependency additions unless already available in repo.
files_expected_to_change: package.json if script wiring is missing
packages/contracts/package.json if screen-contract validation script wiring is missing
ordered_ticket_queue.permission_model: No permission model change unless explicitly stated in ticket scope.
ordered_ticket_queue.gatekeeper_policy: No Gatekeeper behavior change unless explicitly stated in ticket scope.
ordered_ticket_queue.audit_outbox_policy: No audit/outbox behavior change unless explicitly stated in ticket scope.
stop_conditions: registry:verify:phase2 missing and cannot be added within scope
workflow requires secrets
workflow change broadens deployment/release behavior

ordered_ticket_queue.title: Manifest-driven module and capability seed path
ordered_ticket_queue.objective: Seed Phase 2 modules and capabilities from manifests so they are listable, assignable, and usable by runtime authorization.
scope: Seed/list core.access, engagement.gateway, and lead.desk modules.
Seed all manifest-declared capabilities into Capability.
Derive allowed scopes from manifest permissions, not duplicated hardcoded maps.
Allow Access Core to assign Phase 2 capabilities according to manifest scopes.
Preserve Phase 1 access behavior.
files_forbidden_to_change: generated/** except generated registry only if an approved registry step requires it

ordered_ticket_queue.gatekeeper_policy: No Gatekeeper behavior change unless explicitly stated in ticket scope.
ordered_ticket_queue.audit_outbox_policy: No audit/outbox behavior change unless explicitly stated in ticket scope.

Phase 2 capability assignment succeeds for valid scopes

ordered_ticket_queue.title: Gatekeeper support for Phase 2 capabilities
ordered_ticket_queue.objective: Make the real Gatekeeper runtime path support Phase 2 high-risk capabilities instead of relying on mocked service tests.
scope: Support engagement.gateway.request.create, lead.intake.create, lead.status.update, and lead.inbox.assign.
Validate actor, organization, capability, module, entity, request/context consistency, module health, dependency health, and decision token.
Fail closed for deny, degraded_block, approval_required, malformed decisions, mismatches, missing health, and provider errors.
Refactor tests so at least one service-path test per high-risk Phase 2 mutation uses real GatekeeperPreflightService.

ordered_ticket_queue.gatekeeper_policy: Real Gatekeeper path must allow valid Phase 2 preflights and deny invalid/mismatched/degraded decisions.

ordered_ticket_queue.title: Outbox idempotency retry and dead-letter foundation
ordered_ticket_queue.objective: Upgrade EventOutbox foundation for Phase 2 event semantics before module events and gateway/lead workflows depend on it.
scope: Add durable outbox metadata fields such as idempotency_key, attempt_count, next_attempt_at, last_error, dead_lettered_at, locked_at, and locked_by unless repo reality requires a narrower equivalent.
Add service helpers for idempotent event recording and failure metadata.
Keep worker/runtime delivery out of scope unless already present.
Update registry metadata and generated registry.
If behavior changes beyond foundation metadata, create or reference an ADR/subdecision before implementation continues.
non_scope: No event worker implementation unless explicitly required by existing code.
files_expected_to_change: prisma/migrations/** if P2H-001 selected migration baseline
docs/adr/** if ADR/subdecision is required for material outbox retry/dead-letter architecture changes

ordered_ticket_queue.gatekeeper_policy: No Gatekeeper behavior change unless explicitly stated in ticket scope.

existing platform mutation behavior remains compatible

outbox behavior change requires ADR/subdecision and none is available
destructive migration required

ordered_ticket_queue.title: Final Phase 2 hardening gate and external audit package
ordered_ticket_queue.objective: Run full validation and produce a complete external audit package from clean committed hardened branch HEAD.
scope: Run full validation ladder.
Confirm working tree has no tracked source changes before packaging.
Create final source zip using git archive from committed HEAD.
Generate commit log, file list, checksums, validation summary, hardening closure report, known deferrals, and manifest.
Verify excluded paths are absent.
Verify clean status and committed-head basis.
Produce final merge-readiness verdict package.
non_scope: No source implementation except optional tracked closure file if explicitly chosen.
No hidden local/untracked artifacts in source zip.
files_expected_to_change: docs/process/AKTI_ERP_Phase_2_Hardening_Closure_Report_v1.md only if a tracked closure file is intentionally required
ordered_ticket_queue.permission_model: No permission model change unless explicitly stated in ticket scope.
ordered_ticket_queue.gatekeeper_policy: No Gatekeeper behavior change unless explicitly stated in ticket scope.
ordered_ticket_queue.audit_outbox_policy: No audit/outbox behavior change unless explicitly stated in ticket scope.
artifact_rules: Final source zip must be generated from committed branch HEAD.
If all final outputs live under ignored codex-review/, no source commit is required.
If a tracked closure file is required, use docs/process/AKTI_ERP_Phase_2_Hardening_Closure_Report_v1.md exactly and commit it before creating the final archive.

Final external audit package must include source zip, manifest, commit log, file list, checksums, validation summary, closure report, known deferrals, exclusion verification, and clean-status evidence.
stop_conditions: any validation failure
source zip contains excluded paths
working tree dirty with tracked source changes
hardening closure report incomplete
direct WhatsApp/Meta coupling detected
final archive cannot be proven to come from committed HEAD
ordered_ticket_queue.commit_message: P2H-014 produce Phase 2 hardening audit package only if tracked closure file is intentionally created
ordered_ticket_queue.rollback_notes: Final audit artifacts may be ignored; report exact paths and checksums so they can be uploaded for external audit.
explicit_final_package_steps: command -v unzip >/dev/null 2>&1 || stop and report unzip unavailable
if command -v sha256sum >/dev/null 2>&1; then CHECKSUM_CMD=sha256sum; elif command -v shasum >/dev/null 2>&1; then CHECKSUM_CMD="shasum -a 256"; else stop and report checksum utility unavailable; fi
run checksum command over source zip, manifest, commit log, file list, validation summary, closure report, and known deferrals
verify zip file list contains none of: .git, node_modules, dist, .next, .turbo, coverage, codex-review, audits, .env

final_external_audit_package.source_basis: git archive HEAD from committed hardened branch HEAD
must_include: source zip from git archive HEAD
final_external_audit_package.commit_policy: If final outputs are ignored codex-review artifacts only, no source commit is required. If a tracked closure file is intentionally required, it must use docs/process/AKTI_ERP_Phase_2_Hardening_Closure_Report_v1.md and be committed before the final source archive is generated.

authority_model.rule: Only the active v1 repo paths are hardening control authority after P2H-000. Revision-suffixed files are source context only and must not remain as parallel active authority.

P2H_001_gates: P2H-010 when schema persistence is implemented
P2H_003_gates: runtime permission checks that depend on seeded capabilities
P2H_005_gates: P2H-010 when outbox/idempotency coupling is touched

scaffold_only_until_hardened: apps/web package test script currently prints a scaffold message; P2H-012 must replace it with meaningful no-new-dependency tests or stop for explicit dependency/tooling approval.
absent_until_ticketed: prisma/migrations/ is absent until P2H-001 chooses a migration baseline path.
.github/workflows/phase2-validation.yml is absent unless P2H-002 creates a dedicated Phase 2 workflow.
